codeReview.summary: The assigned feature is not actually represented in the reviewed commit. The worker verified an already-landed baseline and made no implementation changes, while commit 3ff4d10 only updates CRUD-surface documentation/comments and generated convenience methods. Because there is no commit diff introducing or tightening the stored-row RecordId decode seam itself, this feature lacks reviewable implementation evidence for its stated behavior.

issues.description: The repository decode seam cited by the worker is pre-existing in the parent commit; commit 3ff4d10 does not change normalize_foreign_shapes, decode_stored_row_value, or any other stored-row RecordId decode logic required by this feature. The reviewed feature therefore has no implementation diff proving the promised stored-row decode seam work.

The RecordId-typed field deserializer attribute is also already present before commit 3ff4d10, and this commit does not modify the macro-generated stored-field decode seam. The feature claim that compatibility was moved under a shared stored-row decode pipeline is unsupported by the reviewed commit history.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker skill does not clearly handle the case where an assigned feature is already satisfied by the mission baseline. That led the worker to mark procedure deviation even though verification-only was the only in-scope action.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff and transcript for session 268e1563-569b-45f2-b3b6-9dbf2d5b5d99 state no code changes were needed and followedProcedure was false only because the baseline already contained the feature.

summary: Fail. I reviewed the feature metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, skill file, mission shared-state files, and commit history. The assigned feature behavior may exist in the baseline, but the reviewed worker/commit did not implement it: commit 3ff4d10 only changes CRUD-surface docs and generated convenience methods, so there is no implementation diff to validate against the feature description.
